questions.question: What distinguishes a combinational logic circuit from a sequential logic circuit?
options: Its output depends solely on its current inputs.
It contains memory elements like flip-flops.
Its output depends on both current inputs and past inputs.
It requires a clock signal to operate.
It is always faster than a sequential circuit.

questions.question: In a virtual memory system, what event typically triggers a 'page fault'?
options: The CPU attempts to access data in a cache block that is not present.
The operating system needs to load a new program into physical memory.
An instruction tries to write to a read-only memory region, causing a protection fault.
The CPU attempts to access a virtual memory page that is not currently mapped to a physical memory frame or is not present in main memory.
A hardware error occurs during a memory write operation.

questions.question: In the context of digital IC design using standard cell libraries, what is the primary purpose of 'library characterization'?
options: To optimize the power grid network across the chip.
To physically arrange standard cells onto the silicon die.
To determine and model the timing, power, and noise characteristics of each standard cell (e.g., gates, flip-flops) under various operating conditions.
To verify the functional correctness of the RTL design through simulation.
To generate the mask layouts for semiconductor manufacturing.

questions.question: In a pipelined or out-of-order processor, which type of data dependency exists when one instruction writes a value to a register, and a subsequent instruction reads from that same register?
options: Write-After-Write (WAW) dependency
Read-After-Write (RAW) dependency
Write-After-Read (WAR) dependency
Control dependency
Structural dependency

questions.question: In digital IC verification, what is the primary role of an 'assertion'?
options: To synthesize high-level C/C++ code into RTL.
To formally prove the equivalence of two design representations (e.g., RTL and gate-level).
To automatically generate test vectors for a design.
To define properties or behaviors that the design is expected to satisfy, often checked concurrently during simulation or formally proven.
To optimize the physical layout of the chip for power consumption.
